title: Why small tools usually win
summary: A short note on why I like tools that do one job clearly instead of trying to become a platform.
body: The best internal tools I use are usually boring. They save a few clicks, remove a little confusion, or make a repeated task easier to finish.

title: Operations and software overlap more than people think
summary: Running a business and building software both come down to clear handoffs, simple systems, and fewer loose ends.
body: Most operational problems are system problems. The same thing is true in code. If the path is unclear, people invent their own paths.

title: Personal sites should be easy to update
summary: A personal site is more useful when changing it feels lightweight enough that you actually do it.
body: If updating a page feels like a project, it probably will not get updated. I want this site to stay simple enough to change quickly.
